exercises.name: EZ Barbell Seated Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grasp an EZ curl bar with a narrow/neutral grip and lift it overhead.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grasp an EZ curl bar with a narrow/neutral grip and lift it overhead.
Hold the bar directly above your head with arms fully extended and elbows pointing forward (upper arms vertical and close to your ears).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar behind your head by flexing only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary and elbows tucked in.
Lower until your forearms are near your biceps or you feel a stretch in the triceps, avoiding excessive shoulder movement or flaring of the elbows.
Exhale and press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are fully straight overhead, maintaining control and avoiding locking aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps while keeping the torso stable, core engaged, and using a controlled tempo to prevent momentum.
exercises.description: Seated overhead triceps extension using an EZ curl bar to isolate the triceps by flexing and extending the elbows while keeping the upper arms stationary. Performed seated to reduce momentum and focus tension on the triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar).
instructions: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar). Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled movement.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral (palms facing) grip and bring your elbows to your sides so your upper arms are vertical and stationary.
Exhale and press the rope down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the rope ends are slightly spread apart at the bottom.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping shoulders down and upper arms fixed; avoid using torso swing or stepping for momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the cable to raise your hands back to the start position, controlling the eccentric phase until your forearms return to vertical.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining tucked elbows and a stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdowns (rope attachment) target the triceps by extending the elbow against resistance while the upper arms remain stationary. The movement also engages the forearms for grip and the delts and abs for posture and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a rope attachment and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a rope attachment and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip so palms face each other and thumbs point inward; elbows should be tucked at your sides and forearms pointing forward with the upper arms nearly vertical.
Inhale, then exhale as you extend your elbows to push the rope downward until your arms are fully straight; at the bottom, separate the rope ends slightly outward to maximize triceps contraction.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping the elbows stationary and wrists neutral.
Slowly control the return (eccentric) phase by allowing the forearms to rise back to the start position until forearms are approximately parallel to the floor or at about 90° elbow flexion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while avoiding torso sway, shoulder elevation, or elbow travel forward/backward.
If you feel shoulder or lower-back compensation, reduce weight, shorten range slightly, or keep a more upright torso and tighter core brace.
exercises.description: Standing cable rope pushdowns isolate the triceps by extending the elbow against a high-pulley resistance; the rope attachment allows a neutral grip and slight outward flare at full extension to emphasize all triceps heads. Keep the elbows fixed at the sides and move only at the elbow joint for controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it straight overhead until your arms are fully extended; this is the start position.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing forward and close to your head, maintaining a stable shoulder position.

Inhale and slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary until your forearms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly, then exhale and press the barbell back up by extending the elbows to return to the fully extended overhead position while keeping the core braced.
Avoid excessive lower-back arching or letting the elbows flare out; if necessary use lighter weight or a spotter to maintain safe form.
After completing your set, have a spotter help lower the bar to your chest/thighs before removing it from your lap, or carefully rack the bar with assistance.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead triceps extension is a single-joint exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows from an overhead position while the upper arms remain stationary. It emphasizes the long head of the triceps and requires core and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Standing French Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ bar overhead with a neutral (angled) grip and hands close to shoulder width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ bar overhead with a neutral (angled) grip and hands close to shoulder width.
Brace your core and keep your chest up and spine neutral; avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Raise the bar overhead so your arms are straight, then slowly lower the bar behind your head by flexing only at the elbows until your forearms are near your triceps or just below ear level.
Keep your upper arms stationary and pointing up (elbows tucked in toward the head); do not let the elbows drift out or flare wide.
Pause briefly at the bottom with a controlled stretch, then press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight (avoid locking out aggressively).
Breathe in as you lower the bar and exhale as you press it up. Maintain a firm grip and neutral wrist position to avoid strain.
Perform repetitions with controlled tempo and stop if you feel excessive elbow or shoulder pain; reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow position or a neutral spine.
exercises.description: Standing overhead French press using an EZ bar to isolate the triceps by flexing and extending the elbows with the bar lowered behind the head. The exercise is performed upright with the upper arms kept vertical and the core braced to prevent lumbar arching.

exercises.name: Cable Standing High Cross Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Set a single handle or rope on a high pulley (above head height).
instructions: Set a single handle or rope on a high pulley (above head height). Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet back with feet hip-width apart.

Grasp the handle(s) with an overhand grip and bring your elbows up and close to your head/upper chest so your forearms are bent and hands are near eye/face level; the cable should be crossed slightly across your body.
Maintain a slight forward lean from the hips, brace your core (neutral spine) and keep upper arms and elbows fixed in place throughout the movement (no shoulder shrugging).
Extend your forearms by straightening the elbows, pressing the handle(s) downward and slightly across your body until your arms are fully extended and triceps are contracted.
Pause briefly at full extension without locking the elbows, then return slowly and under control to the start position with forearms bent.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping tension on the cable, elbows stationary, and avoiding excessive torso movement or swinging.
exercises.description: Standing cable triceps extension performed with the pulley set high and the handle(s) crossed in front of the body; the elbows remain fixed while the forearms extend to straighten the arms, isolating the triceps. Performed with a slight forward lean for stability and controlled tempo on the return.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Prone Alternate Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and lie face down (prone) with your chest and stomach supported and feet braced on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and lie face down (prone) with your chest and stomach supported and feet braced on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (hammer) grip so the palms face each other and the arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Keep your torso pressed to the bench, maintain a neutral spine, and allow the elbows to hang naturally beneath your shoulders (no shoulder shrugging).
Brace your core, then curl one dumbbell by flexing the elbow and bringing the thumb toward the shoulder while keeping the wrist neutral and forearm vertical.
Pause briefly at the top to contract the forearm, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with control.
Alternate arms, performing the same controlled curl with the opposite arm while the other arm remains extended and relaxed.
Breathe out during the curl and inhale as you lower. Avoid swinging the torso or using shoulder movement — focus on isolated elbow flexion.
Choose a weight that allows controlled reps and full range of motion; stop if you feel wrist or elbow pain.
exercises.description: Lying prone on an incline bench, perform alternating hammer curls with a neutral (thumb-up) grip so the forearm muscles (brachioradialis and wrist flexors) do the work. The chest is supported on the bench so movement comes mainly from elbow flexion with minimal shoulder involvement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying extension
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back; grip the barbell with a shoulder‑width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back; grip the barbell with a shoulder‑width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip.
Unrack the bar with a spotter and hold it directly above your chest with arms fully extended and elbows pointing upward (upper arms perpendicular to the floor).
Keeping your upper arms fixed and elbows tucked, inhale and slowly bend the elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead or just behind the head until your forearms reach about 90° to the upper arms.
Pause briefly at the bottom with control (do not bounce off the forehead), then exhale and press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are straight but not locked out aggressively.
Keep wrists neutral and core braced throughout; avoid allowing the elbows to flare forward or the upper arms to move significantly.
Repeat for desired reps and carefully rack the bar with a spotter when finished.
exercises.description: A supine barbell skull‑crusher / lying triceps extension that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow while the upper arms remain stationary above the chest.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (V bar)
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to the high position and attach a V‑bar.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the high position and attach a V‑bar. Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled reps.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip‑width apart and a slight stagger if preferred for balance; grasp the V‑bar with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip.
Start with your elbows bent ~90°, upper arms close to your sides, and a slight forward lean at the hips so your torso is stable and shoulders are over your hips.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms stationary; exhale and press the V‑bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight but not locked aggressively.
Squeeze the triceps at the bottom of the movement for a brief pause (1 second) while maintaining neutral wrists.
Inhale and slowly return the V‑bar to the starting position under control, allowing the elbows to bend until the forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or reach the chosen start depth.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding use of momentum.
Common form cues: keep elbows tucked to your sides (do not let them drift forward or flare out), maintain neutral wrists, and avoid leaning back or swinging the body to complete reps.
exercises.description: Standing cable triceps pushdown using a V‑bar targets the triceps through elbow extension while the V‑grip emphasizes the medial head and allows a neutral wrist position. Performed from a high pulley, the movement is a strict elbow hinge with minimal shoulder movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it straight overhead until your arms are fully extended; this is the start position.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing forward and close to your head, maintaining a stable shoulder position.

Inhale and slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary until your forearms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly, then exhale and press the barbell back up by extending the elbows to return to the fully extended overhead position while keeping the core braced.
Avoid excessive lower-back arching or letting the elbows flare out; if necessary use lighter weight or a spotter to maintain safe form.
After completing your set, have a spotter help lower the bar to your chest/thighs before removing it from your lap, or carefully rack the bar with assistance.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead triceps extension is a single-joint exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows from an overhead position while the upper arms remain stationary. It emphasizes the long head of the triceps and requires core and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Kickback
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend) and keep a neutral spine and neck; this is a bent-over/hip-hinge position.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend) and keep a neutral spine and neck; this is a bent-over/hip-hinge position.
Tuck your elbows tight to your sides so the upper arms are pinned against your torso and forearms hang down (elbows start ~90°).
Brace your core and keep the upper arms stationary — exhale and extend the elbows, driving the dumbbells straight back until the arms are fully extended and the triceps are contracted.
Pause briefly at full extension, keeping the wrists neutral and avoiding shoulder shrugging or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting 90° elbow position with control.
Repeat for desired reps. Maintain hip hinge and neutral spine throughout; reduce weight if you must swing or let the shoulders take over.
exercises.description: Standing bent-over dumbbell kickbacks isolate the triceps by extending the elbow while the torso is hinged forward. The movement is performed with the upper arm held stationary and the forearm driven back until the elbow is straight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; brace your abs and maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; brace your abs and maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp a single dumbbell with both hands by the handle or top plate and lift it overhead so the weight is directly above your head with arms fully extended.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing up and close to your head; avoid letting elbows flare out to the sides.
Inhale and slowly bend your elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly at the bottom without moving your upper arms, then exhale and extend the elbows to raise the dumbbell back to the starting overhead position using the triceps.
Keep wrists neutral and upper arms stationary throughout; do not arch the lower back—maintain core tension to prevent torso movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, selecting a weight that allows controlled movement and full elbow extension without momentum.
exercises.description: Standing overhead dumbbell triceps extension performed with the dumbbell held behind the head; the elbows flex to lower the weight and then extend to drive the weight back overhead, isolating the triceps. Performed standing with core braced to stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Cable Overhead Triceps Extension (rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Setup: attach a rope to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: attach a rope to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.

Starting position: stand upright (feet shoulder-width apart) facing away from the machine, grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and bring the rope ends behind your head so your elbows point up. Step forward slightly to create tension in the cable. Keep a slight bend in the knees.
Brace your core and keep your torso tall — avoid overarching the lower back.
Execution (extend): keeping your upper arms fixed overhead, exhale and extend your elbows to push the rope upward until your arms are nearly straight and the rope ends spread apart.
Top position: squeeze the triceps briefly while maintaining a stable shoulder position (avoid shrugging).
Return (lower): inhale and slowly bend the elbows to control the rope back to the starting position behind your head.
Repetitions: repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining stationary upper arms and controlled tempo throughout.
Form cues and safety: do not flare elbows outward, avoid leaning back to press the weight, and keep the core engaged to protect the spine.
exercises.description: Standing cable overhead triceps extension using a rope attachment; the exercise isolates the triceps by extending the elbows from a position with the rope behind the head while the upper arms remain stationary overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip and wrists aligned with forearms.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip and wrists aligned with forearms.
Keep your elbows tucked to your sides and stationary throughout the movement; avoid letting them drift forward or backward.

Exhale and curl both dumbbells upward by flexing at the elbow, keeping palms facing each other and maintaining a straight wrist; lift until the dumbbells reach near shoulder level and you feel a contraction in the biceps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1 second while keeping the forearms and wrists neutral.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled tempo until arms are fully extended but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding body swing or using momentum; if momentum appears, reduce weight or perform the exercise seated or with back support.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell hammer curl performed with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip to emphasize the biceps while also recruiting the forearm muscles; performed slowly with controlled motion and minimal torso movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Zottman Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart, knees soft, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-forward/hammer) grip at your sides.
instructions: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart, knees soft, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-forward/hammer) grip at your sides.
Keep your elbows tucked to your sides and your torso stable; brace your core to prevent swinging.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up by flexing the elbows while simultaneously supinating the forearms so your palms face up at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top with full elbow flexion and palms facing upward.
Rotate your wrists so your palms face down (pronated) and inhale as you slowly lower the weights under control back to the starting position.
Switch the grip back to neutral at the bottom and repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form and avoiding torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell curl variation that works the biceps during the concentric (curl) while rotating the wrists so the eccentric (lowering) emphasizes the forearms. It combines supination on the way up and pronation on the way down for balanced arm development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Rack Lunge
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and the barbell racked across the front of your shoulders (front-rack) with elbows high and upper arms roughly parallel to the floor; grip the bar with either a clean grip or cross-arm position.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and the barbell racked across the front of your shoulders (front-rack) with elbows high and upper arms roughly parallel to the floor; grip the bar with either a clean grip or cross-arm position.
Brace your core, keep your chest tall and shoulders back to maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Take a controlled long step forward with one foot (standing position to forward lunge), landing on the heel of the front foot.

Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is approximately parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground (about 90° at the front knee); keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid letting it collapse inward.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining an upright torso and tight core; keep the bar stable on the front shoulders by keeping elbows up.
Drive through the front heel to push yourself back to the starting standing position, fully extending the front hip and knee.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch legs and perform the same number of repetitions on the opposite side.
Safety notes: choose a weight that allows you to keep an upright torso and high elbows; if balance is difficult, reduce the load or perform stationary split squats until comfortable.
exercises.description: A loaded lunge performed from a front-rack barbell position that primarily targets the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The bar is held on the front shoulders with elbows high while stepping forward and lowering into a controlled lunge.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
Position feet about hip-width apart and keep a slight, natural bend in the knees (do not lock the knees).
Engage your abs to stabilize the torso and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet until you are fully on your toes and calves are fully contracted; avoid rocking the torso forward.
Pause for 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the step level until you feel a stretch in the calves (control the descent — do not drop abruptly).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping movements smooth and maintaining balance; if needed, reduce weight or use a rail/wall for light support.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed on a raised step while holding dumbbells at the sides to load the lower leg; the movement raises the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). Emphasis is on controlled concentric raise and slow eccentric lowering below the step for stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight. Step under the bar and rest it across your upper back/traps with a secure, pronated grip; unrack the bar and take a small step back.

Position your feet in a split stance: one foot forward (working leg) and the other foot back with toes on the floor and heel elevated slightly; keep a distance so the front knee can bend to about 90 degrees at the bottom.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright and chest lifted throughout the movement; gaze forward to help maintain posture.

Slowly lower your body by bending the front knee and pushing the hips back slightly until the front thigh is around parallel to the floor or you reach a comfortable depth; the rear knee should lower toward the floor but not necessarily touch.
Ensure the front knee tracks in line with your toes (avoid collapsing inward) and keep weight primarily through the front heel and midfoot.
Drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip, returning to the starting split stance. Exhale on the drive.
Complete the desired number of reps on the working leg, then carefully step the back foot forward, re‑rack the bar, reset, and repeat with the opposite leg.
exercises.description: A unilateral, loaded lower‑body exercise performed in a staggered (split) stance with a barbell on the upper back that emphasizes the front leg's quadriceps while also working the glutes and hamstrings for hip control and the core for stability.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Single Leg Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg press seat with your back firmly against the backrest and both hands holding the side handles for stability.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg press seat with your back firmly against the backrest and both hands holding the side handles for stability.
Place the ball of one foot flat and centered on the lower portion of the foot plate so your heel can move freely; keep the other foot off the plate or resting lightly.
Start with the working ankle dorsiflexed (toes pulled toward shin) so the heel is lowered toward the platform.
Press through the ball of the foot by pointing the toes and extending the ankle (plantarflex) until the calf is fully contracted; avoid driving through the knee or lifting the hips.
Pause briefly at full contraction, then slowly lower the heel under control back to the start (dorsiflex) to feel a stretch in the calf.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat. Maintain a controlled tempo and keep the back pressed into the seat throughout.
exercises.description: A single‑leg seated calf press performed on a lever leg‑press machine focusing on ankle plantarflexion to isolate the calf muscles. The exerciser pushes the platform away using the ball of one foot while keeping the torso supported by the seat.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and fasten it to the working ankle; stand beside the machine with feet hip‑width apart.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and fasten it to the working ankle; stand beside the machine with feet hip‑width apart.
Take a small step forward with the inside (support) leg so the working leg is slightly behind and the cable has a straight pull; hold the machine or a handle for balance with both hands.
Maintain a slight forward lean from the hips, keep the torso neutral and core braced, and keep the hips and standing knee stable throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by bending the working knee and driving the heel up toward the glutes, focusing on contracting the hamstrings; avoid lifting the thigh or rotating the hip.
Pause briefly at the top when the heel is close to the glutes and the hamstrings are fully contracted.
Slowly lower the working foot back to the starting position under control until the knee is nearly straight (maintain a soft knee, do not lock out fully).
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch legs. Keep movements controlled and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single‑leg cable curl performed standing with an ankle cuff to isolate knee flexion and target the hamstrings. The exercise is done by curling the heel toward the glutes while keeping the hips stable and torso braced.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and fasten the cuff securely around one ankle (or both if using two cables).
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and fasten the cuff securely around one ankle (or both if using two cables).
Sit upright on a bench or seat with your back against the pad, feet hanging down and knees bent to about 90 degrees; grip the sides of the seat for stability.
Position the cable so that the pulley axis is roughly in line with your knee joint and the cable pulls straight when you extend your leg.
Brace your core (engage abs) and keep your torso still throughout the movement; do not lean back or use momentum.
Exhale and extend the knee by straightening the leg until it is nearly straight; avoid forcefully locking the knee at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for a controlled contraction of the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the leg back to the starting 90-degree knee bend, controlling the eccentric phase until the cable is under tension again.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch legs if working one side at a time. Use a weight that allows smooth, controlled movement and maintain proper knee alignment throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable leg extensions isolate the knee extensors by extending the lower leg against a cable load to target the quadriceps. Performed seated with an ankle cuff attached to a low pulley, the exercise emphasizes controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle cuff to the working ankle; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle cuff to the working ankle; select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine and hold the frame or a handle with both hands for balance; feet hip-width apart, slight forward lean from the hips.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and hips square to the machine throughout the movement.
Begin with a small bend in the standing leg and the working leg slightly behind you with tension on the cable.
Drive the working leg straight back by extending at the hip—focus on squeezing the glute at the top of the movement; keep the knee mostly straight or with a slight bend.
Do not allow the torso to rotate or the lower back to hyperextend; movement should come from the hip joint.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining tension, then control the leg back to the starting position without letting the weight slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: Standing unilateral hip extension performed with a low cable and ankle cuff; extend the working leg backward against resistance to target the gluteus maximus while the hamstrings and lower back assist. The exercise is done standing with torso stabilized and the movement driven from the hip.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Cable Squat
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a single handle or rope to the low pulley.
instructions: Setup: Attach a single handle or rope to the low pulley. Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Grip the handle with both hands and let it hang between your legs so tension is on the cable while standing tall; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core (standing position).

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, keeping your chest up and spine neutral; keep the cable close to your body so tension remains throughout the movement (all fours/awkward positions not required).
Lower until your thighs are about parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows), ensuring knees track over toes and do not collapse inward.
Drive up through your heels and midfoot to return to standing, extending hips and knees while exhaling; keep the cable taut and arms relaxed but stable.
Repeat for the desired reps with controlled tempo on the descent and a steady, powerful ascent; inhale on the way down and exhale as you stand.
Coaching cues/safety: keep heels planted, avoid rounding the lower back, maintain knee alignment over toes, and reduce weight if you cannot keep an upright torso or full tension on the cable.
exercises.description: A squat variation using a low cable for resistance where you face the machine and perform a regular squat while holding the cable handle close to your body. The cable provides constant tension through the movement, targeting the front of the thigh with additional load on the hips and posterior chain.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Cable Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Setup: attach a single handle or straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the machine with feet hip‑width apart; grab the handle with both hands (overhand) and step back until there is light tension in the cable.
instructions: Setup: attach a single handle or straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the machine with feet hip‑width apart; grab the handle with both hands (overhand) and step back until there is light tension in the cable.
Start position: stand tall with a slight bend in the knees, shoulders down and back, chest lifted and core braced (standing, not kneeling or prone), arms extended holding the handle in front of you.

Descent (hip hinge): initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (not by bending the knees more), keeping a neutral spine and neutral head position; allow your torso to lower toward parallel to the floor until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings or until your torso is roughly parallel.
Maintain arm position: keep your arms long and relaxed (the cable provides the load) and avoid pulling with the arms or rounding the upper or lower back.
Ascent (return): drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings, returning to the upright start position while keeping the core engaged and avoiding hyperextension of the lower back.
Breathing and tempo: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward; control the descent and avoid bouncing at the bottom.

Form cues and safety: keep a slight bend in the knees throughout, maintain a neutral spine (do not round the back), keep the cable aligned with the midline of the body, and reduce weight if you cannot keep proper hip hinge mechanics.
exercises.description: A hip‑hinge variation of the Romanian deadlift using a low cable to load the posterior chain; emphasizes hamstring lengthening under tension while the cable provides constant forward pull. Performed standing, keeping a neutral spine and slight knee bend while driving the hips back and then forward.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a handle or hip belt to the low pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: Attach a handle or hip belt to the low pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.

Starting position: Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent (quarter squat), cable/handle positioned at hip level; maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grip/hold: If using a handle, hold it with both hands near the hips or secure the belt around your hips so the cable pulls at hip level.
Perform the thrust: Drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes until your hips are in line with your torso and legs (full hip extension), squeezing the glutes at the top.
Return: Slowly allow your hips to move back to the starting slightly flexed position under control, keeping the core braced and spine neutral.
Repetitions: Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on glute contraction and controlled tempo rather than momentum.
Coaching cues/safety: Keep the torso stable (avoid excessive lumbar arching), maintain a slight knee bend throughout, and do not let the cable pull you off-balance.
exercises.description: A standing hip-extension movement performed against a low cable where you thrust the hips forward against cable resistance to load the glutes. Performed from a slight squat/hip-hinge position and returned under control.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and fasten the cuff securely around one ankle (or both if using two cables).
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and fasten the cuff securely around one ankle (or both if using two cables).
Sit upright on a bench or seat with your back against the pad, feet hanging down and knees bent to about 90 degrees; grip the sides of the seat for stability.
Position the cable so that the pulley axis is roughly in line with your knee joint and the cable pulls straight when you extend your leg.
Brace your core (engage abs) and keep your torso still throughout the movement; do not lean back or use momentum.
Exhale and extend the knee by straightening the leg until it is nearly straight; avoid forcefully locking the knee at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for a controlled contraction of the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the leg back to the starting 90-degree knee bend, controlling the eccentric phase until the cable is under tension again.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch legs if working one side at a time. Use a weight that allows smooth, controlled movement and maintain proper knee alignment throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable leg extensions isolate the knee extensors by extending the lower leg against a cable load to target the quadriceps. Performed seated with an ankle cuff attached to a low pulley, the exercise emphasizes controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the machine for a lying (prone) single-leg curl: adjust the ankle pad so it sits just above the heel when your knee is aligned with the machine pivot.
instructions: Set the machine for a lying (prone) single-leg curl: adjust the ankle pad so it sits just above the heel when your knee is aligned with the machine pivot.
Lie prone on the bench with hips and pelvis flat on the pad, chest supported, and one ankle positioned under the lever pad; grip the front handles or bench for stability.
Keep your torso and hips pressed into the bench (avoid lifting or rotating the hips) and the non-working leg relaxed or slightly bent off the pad.
Exhale and curl the working lower leg by flexing the knee, pulling the pad toward your glutes in a controlled motion; focus on contracting the hamstrings.
Pause briefly and squeeze the hamstrings at the top without jerking or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position until the knee is near full extension, maintaining hip contact with the bench.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat, maintaining controlled tempo and full range of motion throughout.
exercises.description: Prone single-leg curl performed on a lever machine where you curl one lower leg upward against a pad to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings. Performed one leg at a time to reduce compensation and increase unilateral control.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set an appropriate weight on the lever machine and position the ankle/calf cuff or pad behind the working lower leg (just above the heel/ankle).
instructions: Set an appropriate weight on the lever machine and position the ankle/calf cuff or pad behind the working lower leg (just above the heel/ankle).
Stand facing the machine support and lean forward, placing your forearms or chest against the pad/handles so your torso is stable and supported (standing position, body braced).
Place your non-working foot firmly on the floor for support and keep the support-side knee slightly bent to stabilize; the working knee can remain slightly bent as it moves.
Begin with the working thigh roughly in line with your torso (neutral hip). Maintain a neutral spine and avoid arching or rotating the pelvis throughout the movement.
Drive the working leg backward by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is behind the body (do not force hyperextension).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining pelvic stability, then slowly control the lever back to the start position until the hip returns to neutral.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch legs. Keep movement slow and controlled and do not use momentum from the torso or swinging of the leg.
exercises.description: A machine-based single-leg hip extension performed standing with the torso braced on a support; the working leg pushes a lever backward to extend the hip and drive the leg behind the body, emphasizing glute contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
